mechanism_narrative: A 6-month growth acceleration in the US GDP signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in growth acceleration periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a supply-side shock that compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 70% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the unemployment rate signals a labor market slowdown that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in labor market slowdown periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month growth acceleration in the US GDP and a 2-month rise in housing starts signals an expansion in aggregate demand and a boost in construction activity that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in growth acceleration periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 65% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 75% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production and a 2-month rise in the unemployment rate signals a supply-side shock and a labor market slowdown that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side shock and labor market slowdown
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-side shock and labor market slowdown periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 80% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in growth acceleration periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in initial unemployment claims signals a labor market improvement that reduces the risk of a recession and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in labor market improvement periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 70% of instances.
